title: The Third Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## June 30, 2019 | Preacher: Katie Pearson

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Third Sunday after Pentecost in Ordinary Time, falling within the long green season of the church year. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this date would have included 2 Kings 5:1-14 (Naaman's healing), Psalm 30, Galatians 6:1-16, and Luke 10:1-11, 16-20 (the sending of the seventy). This Sunday emphasizes healing, reconciliation, and missional witness themes appropriate to early summer.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was approximately one month away from its 79th General Convention, scheduled for July 8-14, 2019 in Austin, Texas, where significant debates on church polity and social issues were anticipated. In the Diocese of Colorado, Bishop Kimberly Lucas had been leading the diocese since 2015 and would have been engaged in preparations for General Convention. No major controversies were dominating Anglican/Episcopal headlines at this specific moment, though ongoing debates about LGBTQ+ inclusion and liturgical matters continued in various dioceses.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing peak summer weather in late June with temperatures in the 80s and outdoor events in full swing. The week prior saw local attention on Colorado's emerging role in 2020 presidential politics, with Denver hosting Democratic primary debates later that summer. The Capitol Hill neighborhood where the Cathedral sits was undergoing continued gentrification and development pressures, with discussions about neighborhood character and affordability ongoing in city council meetings.

NATIONAL NEWS

The Trump administration was dominating headlines with border and immigration enforcement actions intensifying; reports of overcrowded detention facilities for migrant families were emerging as major news story. The U.S. Supreme Court had just issued decisions on partisan gerrymandering (dismissing challenges) and LGBTQ+ employment discrimination protections in late June. The 2020 presidential campaign was heating up with the second round of Democratic primary debates scheduled for late June in Miami, drawing national media attention.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Iran was the dominant international story as tensions escalated following the U.S. withdrawal from the nuclear deal; the week before this sermon saw Iran announcing it would exceed uranium enrichment limits under the JCPOA. The UK was in political turmoil with Theresa May announcing her resignation as Prime Minister earlier in June, with Boris Johnson emerging as a leading candidate in the subsequent leadership race. Hong Kong was mobilizing for massive pro-democracy protests (which erupted in early July), with tensions rising in late June over the extradition bill controversy.

CULTURAL MOMENT

The summer of 2019 carried a sense of political polarization and anxiety about the future, with the 2020 election looming as a defining moment. Popular culture was dominated by Marvel's "Avengers: Endgame" success (highest-grossing film of all time at that moment), "Game of Thrones" had concluded its controversial final season weeks earlier, and streaming wars were accelerating. Denver's sports culture was focused on baseball season with the Rockies in mid-season, while the Broncos and Nuggets were in their off-seasons; there was general optimism about the Nuggets' young roster after recent playoff success. The broader cultural moment reflected deep divisions on climate change, immigration, and national identity, with younger Americans particularly mobilized on social justice issues.
